records.ety: [L. amen, Gr. 'amh`n, Heb. āmēn certainly, truly.]

defs: An expression used at the end of prayers, and meaning, So be it. At the end of a creed, it is a solemn asseveration of belief. When it introduces a declaration, it is equivalent to truly, verily.
quotes: And let all the people say, Amen.
Amen, amen, I say to thee, except a man be born again, he can not see the kingdom of God.
notes: It is used as a noun, to denote: (a) concurrence in belief, or in a statement; assent; (b) the final word or act; (c) Christ as being one who is true and faithful.

defs: To say Amen to; to sanction fully.
